name: Colosseum
overview: The Colosseum, also known as the Flavian Amphitheatre, is an iconic symbol of Imperial Rome and one of the New Seven Wonders of the World. This massive stone amphitheatre was the largest ever built and could hold up to 80,000 spectators.
history: Built between 70-80 AD under the Flavian dynasty, the Colosseum hosted gladiatorial contests and public spectacles. It remained in use for approximately 400 years, with the last recorded games held in the 6th century.
highlights: Arena floor where gladiatorial battles took place
Multiple levels of seating arranged by social class
Advanced drainage and crowd control systems
tips: Book combined tickets for Colosseum, Roman Forum, and Palatine Hill. Underground and upper tier tours offer unique perspectives. Visit during golden hour for spectacular photos.
